title: 50+ Frequently Asked Questions
description: 50+ expert-answered FAQs on company registration, valuation, FEMA compliance, GST, income tax, forensic accounting, transfer pricing,
bodyText:
Find answers to common questions about our services across all practice areas — company registration, valuation, FEMA compliance, GST, income tax, forensic accounting, transfer pricing, and startup services. Quick Answer: Frequently Asked Questions — 50+ expert-answered FAQs covering company registration, valuation, FEMA, GST, income tax, forensic accounting, transfer pricing, and startup services across India.
Frequently Asked Questions is a service offered by Virtual Auditor, an AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm (IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333) led by CA V. Viswanathan (FCA, ACS, CFE, IBBI RV), from offices in Chennai, Bangalore, and Mumbai since 2012.
Official References: MCA Filing Portal ↗ · SPICe+ Form ↗

faqs.q: How much does Private Limited Company registration cost?
faqs.a: All-inclusive from ₹8,999 covering government fees, DSC, DIN, SPICe+ filing, MOA/AOA, PAN/TAN. No hidden charges. 5–15 working days.

faqs.q: Pvt Ltd vs LLP — which should I register?
faqs.a: Pvt Ltd if you plan to raise VC/angel funding (can issue equity shares). LLP for professional practices and bootstrapped businesses (lighter compliance). OPC for solo entrepreneurs.

faqs.q: How long does company registration take?
faqs.a: 5–15 working days depending on MCA processing times and name availability. SPICe+ integrates PAN, TAN, GST, EPFO, and ESIC in a single application.

faqs.q: What is an OPC (One Person Company)?
faqs.a: A company with a single member and one nominee under Section 2(62) of the Companies Act, 2013. Limited liability protection with simplified compliance.

faqs.q: What is the minimum capital required for Pvt Ltd registration?
faqs.a: No minimum paid-up capital requirement since the 2015 amendment. You can start with as low as ₹1. Authorised capital of ₹1 lakh is common.

faqs.q: Can NRIs register a company in India?
faqs.a: Yes. NRIs and foreign nationals can be directors and shareholders. At least one director must be an Indian resident (stayed in India for 182+ days). FDI reporting via FC-GPR is required.

faqs.q: What annual compliances are required after Pvt Ltd registration?
faqs.a: Annual return (MGT-7A), financial statements (AOC-4), board meetings (minimum 4/year), AGM within 6 months of FY end, DIR-3 KYC for directors, and income tax return filing.

faqs.q: How do I convert my LLP to a Pvt Ltd?
faqs.a: File application under Section 366 of the Companies Act with MCA. Requires NOCs from creditors, consent of partners. Takes 2–3 months.

faqs.q: What is an IBBI Registered Valuer?
faqs.a: A professional registered with IBBI under the IBBI (Registered Valuers) Regulations, 2017. Only Registered Valuers can issue valuation reports under the Companies Act (Section 247) and IBC.

faqs.q: How much does a startup valuation cost?
faqs.a: Pre-revenue valuation from ₹25,000. Growth-stage DCF from ₹50,000. Multi-framework (FEMA + Income Tax + 409A) from ₹1,00,000. 18 methods with Monte Carlo simulations.

faqs.q: How many valuation methods does Virtual Auditor use?
faqs.a: 18 valuation methods including DCF, comparable company analysis, comparable transaction analysis, NAV, venture capital method, option pricing models, and Monte Carlo simulations.

faqs.q: How long does a valuation report take?
faqs.a: Standard: 5–7 working days. Express: 2–3 working days. IBC valuations follow CIRP statutory timelines.

faqs.q: Can you value pre-revenue startups?
faqs.a: Yes. We use Berkus, scorecard, venture capital, and revenue ramp Bayesian methods for pre-revenue companies. 10,000 Monte Carlo simulations model uncertainty.

faqs.q: What is a Section 247 valuation?
faqs.a: Section 247 of the Companies Act, 2013 mandates valuation by an IBBI Registered Valuer for mergers, amalgamations, share buy-back, and other corporate actions.

faqs.q: What is the difference between fair value and fair market value?
faqs.a: Fair value (Ind AS 113) is the exit price in an orderly transaction. Fair market value (Income Tax Act) is the price between a willing buyer and seller with reasonable knowledge.

faqs.q: Do you provide valuation certificates for bank loans?
faqs.a: Yes. We issue IBBI-compliant valuation certificates for collateral assessment, working capital limits, term loan appraisals, and one-time settlement proposals.

faqs.q: What is ESOP valuation?
faqs.a: Fair value of stock options under Ind AS 102 / IFRS 2 using Black-Scholes or Binomial models. Mandatory for Ind AS expense recognition and SEBI SBEB compliance.

faqs.q: What is 409A valuation?
faqs.a: US IRC Section 409A requires independent fair market value determination for stock options granted by companies with US operations. Protects employees from 20% penalty tax.

faqs.q: What is sweat equity valuation?
faqs.a: Valuation of shares issued to employees or directors in exchange for intellectual property or know-how. Governed by Section 54 of the Companies Act and Rule 8.

faqs.q: How is ESOP expense calculated under Ind AS 102?
faqs.a: Grant date fair value × number of options expected to vest, recognized over the vesting period. Black-Scholes for plain options, Binomial lattice for performance-linked ESOPs.

faqs.q: What FEMA services do you provide?
faqs.a: FDI reporting (FC-GPR/FC-TRS), ECB compliance, ODI advisory, LRS guidance, FEMA compounding for late filings, and cross-regulatory conflict detection.

faqs.q: Is DCF the only method for FEMA valuation?
faqs.a: For unlisted companies, yes in practice. Rule 21 of NDI Rules requires internationally accepted pricing methodology but RBI and AD banks consistently require DCF.

faqs.q: What happens if FC-GPR is filed late?
faqs.a: Late FC-GPR constitutes a FEMA contravention under Section 15. Compounding proceedings with potential penalties up to 3× the amount involved.

faqs.q: What is FEMA compounding?
faqs.a: A process under Section 15 of FEMA, 1999 where contraventions are settled by paying a compounding fee to RBI. It regularises the non-compliance.

faqs.q: Can NRIs invest in Indian startups?
faqs.a: Yes, under the automatic route for most sectors. NRIs can invest via FDI (equity), NCD, or convertible notes. FC-GPR filing within 30 days of allotment is mandatory.

faqs.q: What is an ECB (External Commercial Borrowing)?
faqs.a: A loan raised by an Indian entity from a non-resident lender. Requires compliance with all-in-cost ceiling, minimum average maturity, end-use restrictions, and monthly ECB-2 reporting to RBI.

faqs.q: Can you help with GST appeals and show cause notices?
faqs.a: Yes. Section 107 appeals, Section 112 (GST Tribunal), and DRC-01 SCN responses. AI-assisted order analysis extracts demand amounts and computes pre-deposits.

faqs.q: What is the time limit for GST appeal?
faqs.a: Section 107 (Appellate Authority): 3 months. Section 112 (Tribunal): 3 months. Pre-deposit: 10% for first appeal, additional 20% for tribunal.

faqs.q: What is GST registration threshold?
faqs.a: ₹40 lakhs for goods, ₹20 lakhs for services, ₹10 lakhs for special category states. Mandatory registration for interstate supply regardless of turnover.

faqs.q: What is a GST LUT (Letter of Undertaking)?
faqs.a: A declaration filed in Form GST RFD-11 by exporters to make exports without payment of IGST. Valid for one financial year.

faqs.q: How do I reply to a GST show cause notice (DRC-01)?
faqs.a: File response in DRC-06 within 30 days. Include point-wise rebuttal with supporting documents, relevant case law, and reconciliation statements.

faqs.q: What is ITC (Input Tax Credit) reversal?
faqs.a: Rule 42 and 43 of CGST Rules require proportional ITC reversal for inputs used in exempt and taxable supplies. Non-reversal results in demand with 18% interest.

faqs.q: How do I file an income tax appeal?
faqs.a: File Form 35 on the e-filing portal within 30 days of the assessment order. Include grounds of appeal, statement of facts, and supporting documents.

faqs.q: What is Section 263 revision?
faqs.a: The Principal CIT can revise an AO order if erroneous and prejudicial to revenue. Challenge before ITAT within 60 days of the revision order.

faqs.q: What is the penalty under Section 271(1)(c)?
faqs.a: Penalty of 100% to 300% of tax sought to be evaded for concealment of income or furnishing inaccurate particulars. Ambiguous penalty orders are void.

faqs.q: What is presumptive taxation under Section 44AD?
faqs.a: Eligible businesses with turnover up to ₹3 crore can declare 6%/8% of turnover as income. No books of accounts required. Professionals use Section 44ADA (50% of gross receipts).

faqs.q: What is a lower TDS deduction certificate?
faqs.a: Section 197 allows taxpayers to apply for nil/lower TDS where actual tax liability is significantly lower than TDS being deducted. Filed in Form 13.

faqs.q: What is advance tax and when is it due?
faqs.a: Tax payable during the financial year in quarterly instalments: 15% by June 15, 45% by September 15, 75% by December 15, and 100% by March 15.

faqs.q: What is CFE?
faqs.a: Certified Fraud Examiner — a credential from ACFE USA for fraud prevention, detection, investigation, and deterrence.

faqs.q: Do you provide due diligence for startup investments?
faqs.a: Yes. CFE-led financial due diligence for PE/VC investors covering financial statement analysis, fraud risk indicators, and regulatory compliance review.

faqs.q: What is Benford's Law analysis?
faqs.a: A statistical technique used in forensic accounting to detect anomalies in financial data. Deviations from the expected digit distribution signal potential manipulation or fraud.

faqs.q: Can forensic reports be used in court?
faqs.a: Yes. Our forensic reports are prepared to evidentiary standards and have been used in criminal complaints, civil suits, and arbitration proceedings.

faqs.q: What is transfer pricing documentation?
faqs.a: Documentation required under Section 92D for international and specified domestic transactions. Includes Master File, Local File, and CbCR depending on group turnover.

faqs.q: What is the arm's length principle?
faqs.a: Transactions between related parties must be priced as if between independent enterprises. Five methods under Section 92C: CUP, RPM, CPM, PSM, and TNMM.

faqs.q: What is an APA (Advance Pricing Agreement)?
faqs.a: An agreement between taxpayer and CBDT determining arm's length price for future transactions. Valid for up to 5 years with rollback for 4 prior years.

faqs.q: What is DPIIT Startup Recognition?
faqs.a: Recognition under Startup India for companies incorporated for less than 10 years with turnover below ₹100 crore. Benefits include tax exemption under Section 80-IAC.

faqs.q: What is angel tax (Section 56(2)(viib))?
faqs.a: Tax on share premium received by an unlisted company from resident investors exceeding fair market value. Rule 11UA prescribes DCF or NAV methods for FMV determination.

faqs.q: What should a Shareholders' Agreement (SHA) cover?
faqs.a: Board composition, anti-dilution rights, liquidation preference, drag-along/tag-along rights, ROFR, vesting schedules, information rights, and exit mechanisms.

faqs.q: What is a SAFE agreement?
faqs.a: Simple Agreement for Future Equity — converts to equity at a future priced round with a valuation cap and/or discount. Increasingly used in India for early-stage fundraising.

faqs.q: Which cities do you operate in?
faqs.a: Chennai (HQ — Spencer Plaza, Anna Salai), Bangalore (MG Road), and Mumbai (Goregaon West). All services available pan-India.

faqs.q: Do you provide free consultations?
faqs.a: Yes. 30-minute complimentary consultation. Call +91 99622 60333 or email support@virtualauditor.in.

faqs.q: What credentials does CA V. Viswanathan hold?
faqs.a: FCA, ACS, CFE (ACFE USA), and IBBI Registered Valuer (IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333) for Securities & Financial Assets.

faqs.q: Do you offer virtual CFO services?
faqs.a: Yes. Monthly financial reporting, MIS, cash flow management, board meeting support, statutory compliance tracking, and investor-ready reporting.

faqs.q: How do I get a CA certificate for net worth or turnover?
faqs.a: Share your latest audited financials and purpose of the certificate. We issue net worth certificates, turnover certificates, and various certifications for tenders and regulatory filings.
